mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has plateaued near a cycle peak of 5.25% for several months. A sustained pause at a cyclical peak signals the Fed has stopped tightening, reducing forward discount-rate uncertainty and supporting a modest positive drift in equity prices over the next month as markets price in an eventual easing cycle.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS plateau periods (rate unchanged for 3+ months) are not significantly positive versus the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate remains low at 4.6%, near cycle lows. A sudden crossing above a 0.3 percentage-point rise over the trailing 3 months signals an inflection in labor market deterioration, historically preceding equity market weakness as earnings expectations are revised down over the subsequent quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE increases are positive in more than 55% of historical trigger instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) weaken while the federal funds rate remains elevated above 5%. The joint condition of restrictive policy rates and declining housing activity signals compounding interest-rate sensitivity in the real economy, producing negative forward equity returns over the next quarter as the transmission of tight policy to consumer demand becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 5%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with housing downturn
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both trigger conditions are jointly active (compared to baseline), or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series alone (partial Rank IC < 0.02), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating toward flat or negative month-over-month readings signals weakening manufacturing output, which in a late-cycle regime with elevated policy rates leads investors to reprice earnings downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. In early-cycle or recovery regimes the same deceleration is less predictive because it reflects normalization rather than contraction.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: If the negative INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold specifically during high-FEDFUNDS regimes (FEDFUNDS > 5%), i.e., no significant difference in effect size between regimes, or Rank IC < 0.03 in the flagged regime over any 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit a persistent positive month-over-month growth trend, reflecting robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the following week, as strong consumption data reinforces near-term earnings momentum expectations.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant positive association with trailing RSAFS growth (Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window), reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerating while unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously begins rising constitutes a joint labor-market turning signal that is more informative than either series alone, since payroll deceleration alone can reflect noise but combined with rising unemployment indicates a genuine cyclical turn, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market inflection near cycle peak
failure_condition: If the joint-trigger condition does not produce significantly more negative forward returns than either single-series trigger in isolation (interaction Rank IC improvement < 0.02), or overall hit rate for negative returns is below 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where CPI inflation remains elevated relative to the Fed's implicit target while the funds rate is already high, further stickiness in CPIAUCSL month-over-month gains signals persistent inflation risk that constrains the Fed from easing, producing negative equity returns over the next month as rate-cut expectations are pushed out. In low-inflation regimes, CPI upticks have no such effect since policy is not rate-constrained.
regime_hint: high policy rate with sticky inflation regime
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns following the triggered CPI/FEDFUNDS condition are not significantly negative relative to baseline periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1), though reported with a lag, when decelerating on a quarter-over-quarter annualized basis below 2% signals weakening aggregate demand momentum entering the next quarter, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following a sub-2% GDPC1 growth print are not significantly negative compared to periods of stronger growth (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, reject.
